messages.content:
Three ants walk on a thin rod 100 cm long.
When an ant reaches either end of the rod it falls off. When two ants meet,
they BOUNCE: both instantly reverse direction, and each keeps its own speed.

Starting positions and velocities (positive = toward the 100 cm end):

- Ant A: at 10 cm, moving +3 cm/s
- Ant B: at 40 cm, moving -1 cm/s
- Ant C: at 80 cm, moving -2 cm/s

After how many seconds does the LAST ant fall off the rod? Give the exact
number of seconds.


When you are finished, output your final answer on its own line, as the last line of your response, in the exact format:
ANSWER: <your answer>

text:
The ants' bouncing behavior is equivalent to them passing through each other if we ignore their identities. The time each ant would take to fall off the rod if it never bounced is:

- Ant A: distance to right end = 90 cm, speed = 3 cm/s → time = 30 s
- Ant B: distance to left end = 40 cm, speed = 1 cm/s → time = 40 s
- Ant C: distance to left end = 80 cm, speed = 2 cm/s → time = 40 s

The maximum of these times is 40 seconds. A detailed event simulation confirms that the last ants (A and B) fall off the left end at exactly 40 seconds.

ANSWER: 40
